description: The R15 V4 is Yamaha's track-focused sport bike. Aero bodywork, VVA technology, and a rev-happy engine make it the weapon of choice for young enthusiasts. It is not the most comfortable commuter, but on a twisty road, nothing in its class comes close.
cons: Aggressive position is tiring in traffic
Seat is hard for longer rides
Wind blast at highway speeds is significant

common_problems.problem: Aggressive riding position causes wrist fatigue
common_problems.cause: Clip-on handlebars force a forward-leaning posture
common_problems.solution: Normal for sport bikes. Take breaks every 100 km on long rides.

common_problems.cause: Chain needs regular adjustment on sport bikes
common_problems.solution: Clean and lube every 500 km. Check for tight spots at every service.

common_problems.cause: Sport bike design prioritises rider over passenger
common_problems.solution: Aftermarket pillion pad helps. Short rides are fine for pillion.

buying_advice.oem_vs_aftermarket: OEM accessories fit perfectly but cost more. For the R15 V4, aftermarket offers good value for non-critical items like phone mounts and bike covers. For crash protection, go OEM or from a trusted brand.
money_saving_tips: Buy during festive season sales (Diwali, Republic Day) for 15-30% discounts
Start with essentials, add accessories as you identify actual needs
Check Amazon, Flipkart, and local dealers — prices vary significantly
common_mistakes: Buying a cheap helmet to save money
Skipping crash guard — one tip-over can cost thousands in repairs
Using WD-40 as chain lube — it is a cleaner, not a lubricant
Not reading the owner's manual for service intervals
